region_of_origin: Western; served in Singapore café context
food_subgroup: Sweet quick breads and baked loaves

ontology.foodex2_description: Banana bread (quick bread made with mashed bananas)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CJW represents sweet quick breads; used as closest parent as no dedicated banana bread code exists in the published FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for banana bread; concept not present in the SNOMED CT food hierarchy at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical slice of banana bread weighs approximately 90 g, consistent with a standard café serving of a thick-cut loaf slice.

nutrient_source.title: Banana bread, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 18009)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared banana bread used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent a standard banana bread loaf without added butter topping. The Sinpopo serving includes a thick-cut butter slice on the side; butter contribution is noted separately in the notes field but is not included in the per_100g values, which reflect the bread component only. This is the closest approved-source match available; HPB Singapore Foodo does not carry a banana bread entry.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — banana bread entry; corroborated by University of Sydney GI Database banana bread listing (GI ~51, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 51 (glucose scale) is drawn from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Foster-Powell et al. 2002 international GI table (Am J Clin Nutr 76:5–56), which lists banana cake/banana bread at GI ~51. This is treated as an analogous estimate for the Sinpopo sliced banana bread, which is a comparable homemade-style baked banana loaf. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (51 × 46.35) / 100 ≈ 23.7, using 90 g serving × 51.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g = 46.35 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.guidance: Banana bread contains approximately 289 mg sodium per 100 g, and the accompanying thick-cut butter adds further sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one thin slice and request butter on the side or omit it to reduce total sodium and saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 26 g sugar per 100 g and a glycemic load of ~23.7 per standard slice, banana bread can cause a meaningful postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to half a slice, pair it with a protein source to blunt the glycemic response, and account for it within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter and moderate energy density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat in the bread itself (~2.3 g per 100 g) and the additional thick-cut butter served alongside increases total saturated fat intake; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume banana bread occasionally, request minimal or no butter, and be mindful of the overall energy density of 326 kcal per 100 g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Banana bread (sliced) is a moist, dense quick bread made primarily from mashed ripe bananas, wheat flour, eggs, butter, and sugar, leavened with baking soda rather than yeast. It is baked as a loaf and served in thick slices. The Sinpopo Coffee version, available at their Funan outlet in Singapore, is described as having a soft texture with the perfect density to hold its shape, and is served with a slice of thick-cut butter, adding richness and additional saturated fat to the serving. Nutritionally, banana bread is a moderately energy-dense food at approximately 326 kcal per 100 g, with a notable sugar content (~26 g per 100 g) derived from both the ripe bananas and added sugar in the recipe. It provides modest protein (~4.9 g per 100 g) and fat (~11.5 g per 100 g), with a relatively low glycemic index of approximately 51 (low GI category), though the glycemic load per standard 90 g slice is approximately 23.7, which is clinically meaningful for individuals managing blood glucose. The bread contains gluten, eggs, and dairy and is therefore not suitable for those with coeliac disease, egg allergy, or lactose intolerance. It is vegetarian but not vegan. In the Singapore café context, banana bread is commonly consumed as a breakfast item, afternoon snack, or dessert, often paired with coffee or tea. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size and limit the accompanying butter to manage sugar, sodium, and saturated fat intake.
common_user_queries: How many calories in banana bread?
Is banana bread suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of banana bread?
Is banana bread high in sugar?
Can I eat banana bread if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or Singapore-specific nutrient entry exists for banana bread. USDA SR Legacy data for commercially prepared banana bread is used as the closest approved-source match. GI is drawn from the University of Sydney GI Database / Foster-Powell 2002 international table, which lists banana bread/cake at GI ~51. Confidence is medium because the Sinpopo product is a restaurant-specific recipe whose exact formulation is unknown, and the butter accompaniment is not included in the per_100g values.
notes: 1. Per_100g values reflect the banana bread loaf component only, sourced from USDA SR Legacy (NDB 18009 / FDC ID 168880). The thick-cut butter served alongside is not included in these values; consumers should add approximately 35–50 kcal and 2–3 g saturated fat per 5 g butter pat if estimating total intake. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for banana bread. 3. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values are sourced from USDA SR Legacy and are reported as-is; these are -1 only where the source does not report the nutrient. 4. GI of 51 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Foster-Powell et al. 2002 (Am J Clin Nutr 76:5–56) for banana cake/banana bread; this is the closest approved GI source entry. 5. Halal status is listed as false because the Sinpopo Coffee brand has not been confirmed as MUIS-certified halal; consumers should verify directly with the restaurant. 6. FoodEx2 code A0CJW is used as closest_parent as no dedicated banana bread code exists in the published FoodEx2 hierarchy. 7. The serving size of 90 g is an estimate for a standard thick café slice; actual Sinpopo portion weight is not publicly disclosed.
